stem: A electronics testing study measures the battery life (hours) for a sample of 40 smartphone models. The sample mean is 190.6 hours with a sample standard deviation of 24.2 hours. Assuming the data are approximately normally distributed, estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Population mean ≈ 190.6 hours. 95% CI = [182.8, 198.3]. Point estimate: 190.6 hours.

common_failure_modes: Using z-critical value instead of t-critical for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
